events.title: Bacon & Bloodies: Grandma’s Marathon Race Viewing Bar
events.description:
Saturday, June 20 | 8:00 am – 12:00 pm

Open to the public | Cash Bar | Free to attend | Regular parking rates apply
Guests can enter via Entrance D (by the traffic circle).

Imagine holding a Bloody Mary with a healthy strip of bacon without having to fight the crowds. This is a VIP way to celebrate Grandma’s Marathon morning.

Join us to cheer on the Grandma Marathon runners from the perfect perch of the Harbor Roof and Symphony Hall decks. Overlooking Harbor Drive – with a backdrop of Canal Park and the Duluth Aerial Lift Bridge – it’s a beautiful spot for an early summer day. And given that Harbor Drive is one of the last legs of the race and runners truly appreciate your cheers. It’s almost a public service!

Bloody Marys, local beer, wine, and soft drinks will be available for purchase at the cash bar. Credit cards accepted. ATM onsite.

We’re thinking that marathon runners will be passing by the DECC around 9:45 am.

Parking
Regular parking rates apply.
Due to heavy traffic, parking in Canal / Downtown are also options.

Grandma’s Marathon Info
Learn more about the weekend’s line up of events.

venue.description: Event Spaces for All Occasions: Behind the Duluth Entertainment Convention Center (DECC) doors lies 250,000 square feet of meeting and exhibit spaces, beautiful ballrooms and countless meeting rooms. We are the premiere destination convention center directly on the water of Lake Superior. With our ten venues, the DECC is the place to hold a conference or toast your family and friends on a special day, rock out to live music in our arena or intimate auditorium, and cheer your team from curling to dance to hockey to robotics. With sweeping views of Lake Superior, 25 meeting rooms, two ballrooms, two hockey arenas and an emphasis on sustainable practices, plan your next big occasion at the DECC and visit us today!

events.title: Rock the Bayfront Festival
events.description:
When the race is over, the fun’s just beginning on Grandma’s Marathon weekend!

With a move to Duluth’s Bayfront Festival Park the party’s as big and as fun as ever for our participants, volunteers, supporters, and the entire community!

There’s something for everyone at Rock the Bayfront – families and kids included – starting Friday afternoon and going strong through Saturday night, with live music and entertainment at Bayfront Festival Park just beside the waters of the Duluth Harbor.

Whether you’re in need of space to lay out with the family following your big run or a just a fun night out with friends, Grandma’s Marathon weekend has got you covered.

You may come for the run, but you’ll stay for the fun!

venue.description: Bayfront Festival Park is one of Duluth’s most beautiful public parks located on the shores of  Lake Superior.  Visitors to the park can view ships from around the world entering the harbor under the Aerial Lift Bridge. Bayfront Festival Park is home to a variety of events and activities throughout the year.  Enjoy an exciting array of festivals featuring music, entertainment, art and food.  During winter months, there is ice skating and a holiday lighting display. Take a walk around the festival grounds, stage and past the harbor. Play on the biggest, most innovative playground in Duluth. Bayfront Festival Park is a great place for people to go with friends and family almost any time.

events.title: Photography & Art at the Rock: Voyageur Artists
events.description:
June at the Rock is a celebration of creativity, community, and place.

We are proud to feature Voyageur Artists, a collective of local artists from Two Harbors whose work reflects the beauty and character of Minnesota’s North Shore.

This group exhibit brings together a diverse range of styles, perspectives, and mediums — all rooted in a shared connection to the landscapes, waters, and communities that define this region. From painting to photography and beyond, each piece offers a unique interpretation of place.

Voyageur Artists are dedicated to inspiring creativity within the community through exhibitions, workshops, and educational opportunities. Their work not only celebrates the North Shore, but also supports the next generation of artists through scholarships and outreach.

On display June 1–30
Meet the Artists:
Saturdays in June | 10 AM – 6 PM

Experience the North Shore through many lenses — and meet the artists who call it home.

venue.description: Behold 1000 watts of ship-stopping power! Experience the sights, the history, and the technology behind Split Rock Lighthouse, a signature Minnesota landmark anchored on the rocky coast of Lake Superior. Join MNHS in commemorating the SS Edmund Fitzgerald each year on the anniversary of its sinking in Lake Superior. Share a moment of reflection as the lighthouse shines for the 29 mariners lost on that fateful day in 1975 — and all those who have perished on Lake Superior. Buildings/Grounds, Visitor Center/Museum Store, Fog Signal Building & Lighthouse open only during site hours. Free parking for Historic Site visitors during site hours only. A State Park vehicle permit is required during off hours.

events.title: Duluth River Train Excursion
events.description:
Ride the Lake Superior and Mississippi Railroad. A historic 2-hour rail journey through the majestic St. Louis River Estuary. Family-friendly, volunteer-run, and purely Duluth. Our narrated tour takes you along the original Lake Superior & Mississippi line in vintage coaches. Go where cars and hiking boots can’t reach. The St. Louis River Estuary is a hidden sanctuary for bald eagles, osprey, and Northland wildlife.

Bring your camera for unparalleled views of the Duluth riverfront. Best to arrive about a half-hour before departure.

Our ticket office is located just off of Grand Avenue.

events.title: Flamingo Fest
events.description:
Birds of a feather flock together!

We’re kicking off summer with our 5th Annual Flamingo Fest — a wacky, wild, and wonderfully weird way to pre-game or post-party Grandma’s Marathon!

Looking for a chill local getaway? Lincoln Park’s got shops, sips, and bites galore. Want to dance ‘til your wings fall off? Or just chillax in the yard to some vibey tunes and rest your legs. We’ve got that too.

Grab your flock, stretch those legs, and let’s get flamingo fabulous.

Bent Paddle Taproom Event: 3 PM – 10 PM

Live Music: 4 PM – 8 PM
4:30p ~ Willem DaFoe Fan Club
6:30p ~ Entheos Enclave

Food Partner:
Bowlz N Thangz – With themed bowlz and bites that will have the whole town flocking to the yard!

BP Glam Throwdown:
Break out your flamingo-themed or tropical best and strut your stuff! Fabulous, flamingo-inspired prizes await the most dazzling participants.

All Ages | Family Friendly

events.title: Science Riot: Scientists Doing Standup?!
events.description:
Get ready to laugh your lab coat off as Duluth-area scientists try standup comedy for the first time!

Join us for a night of laughter, learning, and libations as Science Riot brings its uproarious, in-person comedy show to the Zenith City! After surviving a crash course in comedy, local scientists trade their microscopes for the microphone and deliver hilarious takes and serious insights into their real-life work. Whether you’re a science nerd or just love a good laugh, this is the place to learn the weird and wild secrets of science!

Science Riot is a 501(c)3 nonprofit that teaches scientists how to do public outreach through standup-style comedy about their field of expertise. We help STEM folks make science accessible, relatable and engaging. The results speak for themselves: think late night monologue meets TED talk!

Get more information about our program.

venue.description: The Zeitgeist Arts building is a hub for film, food, art, and community in downtown Duluth. We are your home movie theater, performance theater, art gallery, and restaurant & bar. We thrive on connecting creatives and community members. We are a nonprofit arts and community development organization committed to growing and sustaining a community that is inclusive, diverse, and equitable; artistic and vibrant; environmentally conscious; and a place where every individual can thrive. Working in partnership across our region, Zeitgeist connects like-minded organizations and people to collaboratively create programs that make lasting change.

events.title: Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
events.description:
Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior.

Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine.

Whether you’re celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences.

Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening.

Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

venue.description: Experience the wonders of Lake Superior as we cruise next to 1,000-foot lake freighters and oceangoing vessels to take in the activities of the bustling harbor. The Vista Fleet is the Excursion Boat Company and (only) Floating Events Center based in Duluth, Minnesota. The Fleet consists of: The Vista Star (100 ton vessel) that holds up to 220 passengers, The Vista Queen (46 ton vessel) that holds up to 50 passengers and the rebranded Zenith Adventure Gift Shop (located behind the DECC/Amsoil Arena) which is a 5,000 square gift shop and Adventure company. The cruises are fully live narrated with a blend of entertaining facts, games, concessions, a full bar and much more. Join us for a dinner, lunch, or family fun cruise!

events.title: Summer Solstice High Tea
events.description:
Step into an enchanting afternoon of tea, magic, and mysticism at Ritual Salad and Marketplace’s Solstice High Tea experience. Sip your choice of handcrafted tea while enjoying a decadent entrée and dessert as live music fills the room. Throughout the event, a curated panel of mystical women will explore the worlds of tarot, psychic intuition, astrology, spell work, and more through interactive discussion and audience Q&A. Guests will also have the opportunity to mingle with the practitioners throughout the afternoon and book optional one-on-one sessions for a more personal experience.

Tickets are $65 and include tea service, entrée, and dessert. Join us for an unforgettable afternoon of connection, curiosity, and magic!

June 21st, 2026
Doors open at 12:30pm, tea service begins at 1:00pm.
Seating is limited so be sure to book your tickets today!

events.title: Sunday Night at the Races
events.description:
Join us (almost) every Sunday night in Proctor for the races!

Hear the rumble and feel the dust fly as our local dirt track racers put on a show for the fans all summer long! Bring your family to enjoy a Sunday evening outside with plenty of room in bleachers, concession stands, accessible fully running restrooms and a fun atmosphere. Grab a friend and try something new!

Check out our Facebook page to stay up to date with current events, cancellations due to weather and special events coming to our track now through September. (Search “Proctor KME Speedway” on Facebook.)

The cars hit the track at 4:30 to warm up, but the green flag drops at 5pm. Join us and help keep dirt track racing in the northland alive!

Admission (Regular Race Night)
Adults: $15
Students (13-18) & Senior Citizens: $10
Child (6-12): $2
Under 5: Free
Pit Admission: $30

Special Events
Saturday, August 1st: Midsummer Night of Mayhem School Bus Races (Rain day August 2nd.)
Sunday, August 23rd: Season Championship (Rain day August 30th.)
Thursday, September 3rd: 54th Annual Silver 1000 (Premiere racing event! Rain day September 9th.)
Wednesday, September 9th: Wednesday Nighters Car Show

events.title: Forest Bathing at the Lake Superior Zoo
events.description:
Monday, June 22, 2026 from 9:30-11:30 a.m.

Explore and be inspired while forest bathing at the Lake Superior Zoo.

Different from a nature hike, being fully in the present moment gently awakens awe and discovery. Technology is set aside to plug into the healing and calming power of nature. During the 2 hour session a series of guided invitations are intermixed with walking slowly, sitting in silence, mindfully observing nature, and sharing what is witnessed. There is a mix of solo and group time.

The session concludes with tea and light snacks. Return home to your place of belonging as part of what is wonderfully wild.

$40 per person, or $70 for one youth and accompanying adult. Pre-registration with payment required.

events.title: High Key Mondays & Industry Nights
events.description:
High Key Mondays & Industry Night Are BACK!
Live local music | Drink specials | 6–9pm

High Key Monday is FRI-YAY for many of our industry partners — and we’re roll with it. Calling all restaurant, bar, liquor store, and brewery professionals in the Twin Ports — get on down to the Paddle for Industry Night!

Show a worker ID or pay stub for $1 off all Bent Paddle beverages. Thank you for keeping our paddles in the water!

Everyone is welcome! Come for the tunes, stay for the conversations, delicious beverages, and good times.

This month to kick off and celebrate the return, we invite you to connect with local organizations making waves in the Twin Ports — like WE Health, Trans+ Northland, Twin Ports Artists Collective, and Men As Peacemakers. Org’s may vary based on who available each week, but the good vibes are guaranteed!

events.title: The Roadside Lupines
events.description: The Roadside Lupines are a 4-piece folk band based out of Duluth, Minnesota. After meeting in college, the band came together in 2023, blending heartfelt original songs with soulful covers spanning from Duluth’s own Trampled by Turtles to Shania Twain. With a name and music rooted in the North Shore, they capture the spirit of life up north and the place they are grateful to call home. The Roadside Lupines released their debut album at the tail end of 2025! Check it out wherever you get your music.

events.title: $10 Tuesday on the Vista Fleet
events.description:
Climb onboard for one of Duluth’s favorite summer traditions, $10 Tuesdays are back at the Vista Fleet!

Enjoy live-narrated sightseeing cruises through the Duluth-Superior Harbor for just $10 per ticket, all day long, every Tuesday through Labor Day. Reserve your tickets in advance as tours DO sell out.

Take in stunning views of Lake Superior, the Aerial Lift Bridge, and the working waterfront from the comfort of the Vista Fleet. Concessions and a full bar are available onboard, so grab a drink, kick back, and enjoy a midweek escape on the water.

Book your tickets online, no promo code needed!

venue.description: Experience the wonders of Lake Superior as we cruise next to 1,000-foot lake freighters and oceangoing vessels to take in the activities of the bustling harbor. The Vista Fleet is the Excursion Boat Company and (only) Floating Events Center based in Duluth, Minnesota. The Fleet consists of: The Vista Star (100 ton vessel) that holds up to 220 passengers, The Vista Queen (46 ton vessel) that holds up to 50 passengers and the rebranded Zenith Adventure Gift Shop (located behind the DECC/Amsoil Arena) which is a 5,000 square gift shop and Adventure company. The cruises are fully live narrated with a blend of entertaining facts, games, concessions, a full bar and much more. Join us for a dinner, lunch, or family fun cruise!

events.title: Leadership Duluth
events.description:
Leadership Duluth is a comprehensive 10-month leadership development program by the Duluth Area Chamber of Commerce that aims to unite, engage, and empower leaders to foster change within their workplace and community.

Throughout the program, participants examine core leadership competencies, learn about Duluth’s cultural and business history, and discuss current issues facing the region. As a result, participants grow to understand the elements of a strong community and how their leadership can create opportunities within a business and cultivate a robust economy.

More than 1,500 area professionals have completed the Leadership Duluth program since its first graduating class in 1988.

For more information, visit leadershipduluth.com

We are the premiere destination convention center directly on the water of Lake Superior. With our ten venues, the DECC is the place to hold a conference or toast your family and friends on a special day, rock out to live music in our arena or intimate auditorium, and cheer your team from curling to dance to hockey to robotics. events.title: Storytime on the Irvin
events.description:
Hosted by the Duluth Public Library

Storytime ho! Climb aboard the William A. Irvin historical ship for a special storytime! We’ll meet on the top deck for stories, songs, and rhymes at 9:30am, followed by time for families to explore the ship. For kids pre-K – grade 3 with their caregivers.

Due to its historical nature as a retired freighter, the Irvin is not ADA accessible and includes multiple staircases to access certain locations. No strollers or wagons please. The top deck has minimal barriers, children must be monitored at all times.

When you register, please indicate how many people will be in your group. Ship deck capacity is 130 people.

Parking will be free for this event in the DECC parking lot across from the ship. Please let parking booth attendant know you are here for Storytime on the Irvin and they will honor complimentary parking.

Register and learn more at duluthlibrary.events.mylibrary.digital

venue.description: Come aboard! In the heart of Canal Park, set against the majestic Duluth Aerial Lift Bridge, is a Great Lakes vessel you can’t miss — and won’t want to. Despite carrying iron ore and coal through treacherous storms, the William A. Irvin remains in pristine condition today. Don’t miss your chance to tour the proud flagship of U.S. Steel’s Great Lakes Fleet. Whether you’re interested in a 2,000 horsepower steam turbine engine or delicate antique fixtures, you’ll find them both in shipshape. The Irvin is docked along the waterfront of Lake Superior next to the Duluth Entertainment Convention Center (DECC) and near the Aerial Lift Bridge.

events.title: Beer Garden at Glensheen
events.description:
Join us at Glensheen for a unique Beer Garden experience, perfect for beer-enthusiasts and family fun on the estate grounds. Taste local craft beer, ice cream, food provided by local food trucks, while spending time with friends and family and taking in breathtaking Lake Superior views throughout Wednesday evenings in June. Tune in at 6:30pm as our local brewers share their stories during Brew Talks.

2026 Lineup

Wednesday evenings in June from 5pm – 8pm.

June 3: Bent Paddle Brewing Company
June 10: Earth Rider Brewery
June 17: Castle Danger Brewery
June 24: Ursa Minor Brewing

Food & Drinks

One food truck option will be available each night for the Beer Garden: Gumbeaux Guys or Oasis Del Norte. Local ice cream from Love Creamery, small snacks, and alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks will also be available for purchase on-site.

events.title: Lake Superior Live Cruise ft. Abe Curran
events.description:
Relax on Lake Superior with live local music by Abe Curran, great food, and unforgettable waterfront views.

Lake Superior Live is Vista Fleet’s summer music series, combining a casual harbor cruise, live performances from some of the Twin Ports’ favorite musicians, and a BBQ-style dinner. Whether you’re looking for a unique date night, a fun evening with friends, or simply an excuse to get out on the water, this cruise offers a laid-back way to enjoy Duluth’s waterfront.

Weather permitting, cruises travel through both the Duluth Canal and Superior Entry, offering incredible views of Lake Superior, working waterfront activity, and the Duluth skyline. Your ticket includes a casual BBQ dinner. Grab a drink from the bar, find your favorite seat, and enjoy an evening of live music as the sun begins to set over the harbor.

A full bar is available onboard with beer, wine, cocktails, soft drinks, and non-alcoholic options available for purchase.

venue.description: Experience the wonders of Lake Superior as we cruise next to 1,000-foot lake freighters and oceangoing vessels to take in the activities of the bustling harbor. The Vista Fleet is the Excursion Boat Company and (only) Floating Events Center based in Duluth, Minnesota. The Fleet consists of: The Vista Star (100 ton vessel) that holds up to 220 passengers, The Vista Queen (46 ton vessel) that holds up to 50 passengers and the rebranded Zenith Adventure Gift Shop (located behind the DECC/Amsoil Arena) which is a 5,000 square gift shop and Adventure company. The cruises are fully live narrated with a blend of entertaining facts, games, concessions, a full bar and much more. Join us for a dinner, lunch, or family fun cruise!

events.title: Pier B Courtside Concerts
events.description:
June 17th: Summer Kickoff & 10 Year Celebration
Celebrate a decade of lakeside memories and the start of the best season of the year! We are thrilled to invite you to the Pier B Summer Kickoff Celebration on June 17th, a special edition of our Courtside Concerts honoring Pier B Resort Hotel’s 10-Year Anniversary. Set right on the water’s edge at the Pier B Sport Court, this evening promises the perfect Duluth summer vibe. Whether you’re here for the music by Aftershock, the BBQ and beverages, or the breathtaking harbor views, there’s no better place to ring in the season.

Live Music Most Wednesdays
Make plans for a summer night of live music on the water most Wednesdays! Grab your crew, order a drink, and enjoy the music with unbeatable views of Lake Superior. Check our event calendar for the full lineup.

We hope to see you there!

Food and beverage available for purchase.

venue.description: At Clyde Iron Works, we take pride in our rich history and the role that our space had in shaping both industry and community around the US. Originally founded in 1889 as a steel fabrication plant, our building was once at the heart of American innovation, helping construct major infrastructure projects worldwide. Today, we’ve transformed this historic space into a one-of-a-kind gathering place where history meets hospitality. Whether you’re here for a meal, a celebration, or a live event, we invite you to experience the energy, character, and craftsmanship that make Clyde Iron Works truly special.

organizer.description:
Girl Scouts create the world they want to live in and strive to make it better every single day. They explore their strengths, take on new challenges, and can always be themselves, regardless of background or ability.

events.title: Science on Deck
events.description:
The Scientists and Staff of UMD’s Large Lakes Observatory (LLO) invite the community to board and tour the Blue Heron Research Vessel.

Science on Deck events are free and open to the public from 11 am–3 pm. Enjoy guided tours of the research vessel from the crew, interact with graduate students working on freshwater research, and explore engaging displays.

The science presentation at 1:30 pm on June 26 will be from our Large Lakes Observatory’s Director, Dr. Catherine O’Reilly, titled: “Underwater Soundscapes.” The R/V Blue Heron will tie-up at the Pier B Resort for this event, 800 W. Railroad Street, Duluth, MN.

ABOUT LLO:

Located on the Duluth- campus the the University of Minnesota Duluth, LLO is the only institute in the country dedicated to the study of large lakes throughout the world. They focus on the global implications of investigations in the areas of aquatic chemistry, circular dynamics, geochemistry, acoustic remote sensing, plankton dynamics, sedimentology and paleoclimatology.

The Large Lakes Observatory serves as a basis for assessing human impact on large-lake ecosystems, and for developing sound policy for protecting these invaluable bodies of freshwater as our global environment evolves.

events.title: Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
events.description:
Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior.

Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine.

Whether you’re celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences.

Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening.

Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

events.title: “Weird Al” Yankovic: Bigger & Weirder 2026 Tour
events.description:
Special Guest: Puddles Pity Party

 

TICKETS

Tickets on sale now!

 

VIP Meet & Greet and Backstage Tour Packages are available for this show. Click HERE for more
information on VIP packages.

 

PRICING:

P1 – $175.50 + fees = $199.20

P2 – $155.50 + fees = $178.75

P3 – $125.50 + fees = $148.05

P4 – $95.50 + fees = $117.35

P5 – $75.50 + fees = $96.90

P6 – $55.50 + fees = $76.45

P7 – $45.50 + fees = $66.20

P8 – $35.50 + fees $56.00

 

 

ABOUT “WEIRD AL” YANKOVIC

Weird Al brings his legendary full-production multimedia comedy rock show back to the concert
stage with the BIGGER & WEIRDER 2026 Tour, playing his iconic hits as well as some never-
performed-live-before fan favorites. Al’s long-time band is joined by four additional players to
create a super-sized concert experience.

“Weird Al” Yankovic is the biggest-selling comedy recording artist in history. A 5-time Grammy
Award winner, he is best known for his parodies of the biggest musical artists over the last 4
decades, including Madonna, Michael Jackson, Taylor Swift, Queen, U2 and Lady Gaga, all with
their personal blessings. His many hits include “Amish Paradise,” “Eat It,” “Like a Surgeon,”
“Smells Like Nirvana,” “Word Crimes,” and the platinum-selling “White & Nerdy.” His last
album Mandatory Fun is the only comedy album in history to debut at #1 on the Billboard Top
200. Yankovic’s live shows have entertained audiences across the globe for generations,
including appearances at such iconic venues as Radio City Music Hall, The Hollywood Bowl, Red
Rocks, Carnegie Hall, Wolf Trap, The Kennedy Center and Madison Square Garden. In 2022,
Yankovic produced and co-wrote the Emmy-winning biopic WEIRD: The Al Yankovic Story,
starring Daniel Radcliffe in the title role.

 

 

ABOUT PUDDLES PITY PARTY

Puddles Pity Party, the 7-foot sad clown whose golden voice is “comparable to any Grammy
winner” (Los Angeles Times), has amassed over 900K YouTube subscribers and performed sold
out shows around the globe including San Francisco’s Palace of Fine Arts, London’s Soho Theatre
and a residency at Caesars Palace in Las Vegas. He has performed on stage with such legends as
Eric Idle, Nick Offerman, Jack Black, Maynard James Keenan and Primus. Puddles’ one-of-a-kind
“textured voice laced with melancholy” (NY Times) has been hailed as “operatic” (Boston Globe)
and his show both “life-affirming” (Herald Scotland) and “hysterically funny” (LA Weekly).

Duluth is a playground of the Twin Cities and the metro destination of the North, making it the primary vacation location of residents in the St. Paul and Minneapolis areas. With sandy beaches and lake breezes, craft brewery culture and – of course – entertainment, Duluth is a cool city.
venue.address: Amsoil Arena, Harbor Drive, Duluth, MN, USA

organizer.description: Behind the Duluth Entertainment Convention Center (DECC) doors lies 250,000 square feet of meeting and exhibit spaces, beautiful ballrooms and countless meeting rooms. We are the premiere destination convention center directly on the water of Lake Superior. With our ten venues, the DECC is the place to hold a conference or toast your family and friends on a special day, rock out to live music in our arena or intimate auditorium, and cheer your team from curling to dance to hockey to robotics. With sweeping views of Lake Superior, 25 meeting rooms, two ballrooms, two hockey arenas and an emphasis on sustainable practices, plan your next big occasion at the DECC and visit us today!

events.description: Held on the sandy stretch of Minnesota’s longest sandbar, the Park Point Art Fair (PPAF) is a weekend celebration of fine art, creativity, and community. With over 100 juried artists showcasing work in ceramics, painting, jewelry, sculpture, photography, fiber, wood and more, this beloved event draws art lovers from across the region. Visitors can enjoy live music, food trucks, hands-on art activities, and an accessible layout that makes it easy to explore every booth. Proceeds support local youth, environmental efforts, and civic projects through the Park Point Community Club — making this art fair as impactful as it is inspiring.

events.title: Photography & Art at the Rock: Voyageur Artists
events.description:
June at the Rock is a celebration of creativity, community, and place.

We are proud to feature Voyageur Artists, a collective of local artists from Two Harbors whose work reflects the beauty and character of Minnesota’s North Shore.

This group exhibit brings together a diverse range of styles, perspectives, and mediums — all rooted in a shared connection to the landscapes, waters, and communities that define this region. From painting to photography and beyond, each piece offers a unique interpretation of place.

Voyageur Artists are dedicated to inspiring creativity within the community through exhibitions, workshops, and educational opportunities. Their work not only celebrates the North Shore, but also supports the next generation of artists through scholarships and outreach.

On display June 1–30
Meet the Artists:
Saturdays in June | 10 AM – 6 PM

Experience the North Shore through many lenses — and meet the artists who call it home.

venue.description: Behold 1000 watts of ship-stopping power! Experience the sights, the history, and the technology behind Split Rock Lighthouse, a signature Minnesota landmark anchored on the rocky coast of Lake Superior. Join MNHS in commemorating the SS Edmund Fitzgerald each year on the anniversary of its sinking in Lake Superior. Share a moment of reflection as the lighthouse shines for the 29 mariners lost on that fateful day in 1975 — and all those who have perished on Lake Superior. Buildings/Grounds, Visitor Center/Museum Store, Fog Signal Building & Lighthouse open only during site hours. Free parking for Historic Site visitors during site hours only. A State Park vehicle permit is required during off hours.

events.title: Duluth River Train Excursion
events.description:
Ride the Lake Superior and Mississippi Railroad. A historic 2-hour rail journey through the majestic St. Louis River Estuary. Family-friendly, volunteer-run, and purely Duluth. Our narrated tour takes you along the original Lake Superior & Mississippi line in vintage coaches. Go where cars and hiking boots can’t reach. The St. Louis River Estuary is a hidden sanctuary for bald eagles, osprey, and Northland wildlife.

Bring your camera for unparalleled views of the Duluth riverfront. Best to arrive about a half-hour before departure.

Our ticket office is located just off of Grand Avenue.

events.title: Book Signing: Pearl’s Garden by Carolyn Olson
events.description:
Join author and illustrator Carolyn Olson for a signing of her new children’s book, Pearl’s Garden, at Split Rock Lighthouse.

As young Pearl tends to her urban garden in the tradition of her mother and grandmother, her dedication and generosity inspire family members to share in the work and reap the rewards.

Written and illustrated by Carolyn Olson, Pearl’s Garden celebrates the power of urban gardens to bring people together — to share memories, to raise plants, and to savor fresh flavors.

Carolyn Olson is a narrative artist working and living in northern Minnesota. She uses gouache, oil, and pastel to depict everyday life stories. Her “Essential Workers” series celebrates the work carried on during the COVID-19 pandemic. She lives in Duluth, Minnesota.

Get your copy of the book signed and meet Carolyn Olson. Books will be available for purchase.

venue.description: The North Shore Scenic Railroad offers train excursions in Duluth, Minnesota in the spring, summer, fall, and holiday season. Train excursions depart daily from the Duluth Depot in summer, with short rides for all ages, kids events, and elegant dinner trains for grown ups.

events.title: Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
events.description:
Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior.

Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine.

Whether you’re celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences.

Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening.

Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

venue.description: Experience the wonders of Lake Superior as we cruise next to 1,000-foot lake freighters and oceangoing vessels to take in the activities of the bustling harbor. The Vista Fleet is the Excursion Boat Company and (only) Floating Events Center based in Duluth, Minnesota. The Fleet consists of: The Vista Star (100 ton vessel) that holds up to 220 passengers, The Vista Queen (46 ton vessel) that holds up to 50 passengers and the rebranded Zenith Adventure Gift Shop (located behind the DECC/Amsoil Arena) which is a 5,000 square gift shop and Adventure company. The cruises are fully live narrated with a blend of entertaining facts, games, concessions, a full bar and much more. Join us for a dinner, lunch, or family fun cruise!
